daily.title: BTC Holds $62K — Alts Show Oversold Bounce Setup

sections.heading: Market Overview
sections.content: BTC is holding above $62,000 after triggering a 1h buy signal at $62,740 (SL $62,308, TP $63,389, R:R 1.5:1). The broader market is recovering from June's correction. Altcoin Season Index sits at 49/100 — right on the borderline between Bitcoin Season and Altcoin Season. Fear & Greed at 19 (Extreme Fear), historically a contrarian accumulation zone.

sections.heading: BTC Dominance Watch
sections.content: BTC dominance is testing critical support at 57-59% after breaking down from a multi-year rising channel. A breakdown below 57% could signal the start of a sustained altcoin season. Watch this level closely — it's the single most important macro signal right now.

sections.heading: Key Levels
sections.content: BTC: Support $62,300 | Resistance $63,400. A clean break above $63,400 opens path to $65,000. Failure to hold $62,300 could retest $60,000.

sections.heading: Volume Note
sections.content: Volume across the board is dry at 0.3-0.5x average. Light sizing is advised — entries may take time to fill in low-liquidity conditions. The next major volume catalyst could be US session open.

sections.heading: Watchlist
sections.content: SOL, FET, AVAX showing deeply oversold 1h RSIs (23.8, 24.5, 30.9 respectively). These are prime bounce candidates if BTC maintains support. INJ and SUI also within value area with oversold 1h readings.

weekly.title: Market Recovers from June Selloff — Rotation Building

sections.heading: Weekly Performance
sections.content: BTC: +3.2% — recovered from June low of $58,200 to current $62,740. ETH: +1.8% — lagging but holding structure. Top performers: ADA (+7.06%), BCH (+4.98%), ETHFI (+12.25%). The recovery is broad but low-volume, suggesting institutional accumulation rather than retail FOMO.

sections.heading: Macro Context
sections.content: The broader macro environment remains challenging. Hawkish Fed stance, geopolitical tensions, and record ETF outflows drove June's correction. However, Extreme Fear readings (Fear & Greed at 19) have historically preceded significant market reversals. BTC dominance breaking down from its multi-year channel is the most notable technical development this week.

sections.heading: Sector Rotation
sections.content: AI tokens showing relative strength despite the downturn. FET has held structure better than most alts. Layer 1s (ADA, SOL) leading the recovery bounce. DeFi tokens lagging — not yet participating. Watch for rotation from BTC dominance into large-cap alts first, then mid-caps.

sections.heading: Week Ahead
sections.content: Key levels to watch: BTC $62,300 support and $63,400 resistance. A breakout above $63,400 with volume would be strongly bullish. The altcoin season index at 49 makes this a pivotal week — a push above 50 would confirm rotation into alts. Watch for US economic data mid-week that could drive volatility.

sections.heading: Positioning
sections.content: Our Hermes Portfolio started with $1,000 this week and closed at $1,087 (+8.74%). 4 trades, 3 wins, 1 loss (75% win rate). Best trade: SOL long +$52.10. Current open position: SOLUSDT long at $79.50, currently +$2.54. Light sizing (20% per trade) appropriate for current volume conditions.
